story:
Dear Diary,
Melanie says Omar says Anthony says he has a crush on me, but he never talks to me. Melanie says that’s what boys do when they like you. Not talk. She’s four months older and says it matters. Maybe it does. Maybe it doesn’t.

Dear Diary,
Richard dared Kevin to eat a worm at recess. Kevin said he would if Richard did, so they both did, and then they both threw up in class. No double dog dare would make me eat a worm. Also, I tried to see if Anthony was looking at me, but Mr. Silvio caught me and made me write “I will always pay attention” twenty times. I told him nobody always pays attention and he said that wasn’t true. I told Dad and he said I shouldn’t have said that, even if it’s true. That sometimes you keep things to yourself. I asked if that’s the same as lying. Dad said no, it’s just staying quiet. It still feels like a kind of lie. I don’t know.

Dear Diary,
Melanie says when we grow up we have to get married. I said we should marry each other. She said no, we have to marry boys. I don’t know if I want to marry a boy. I like that Melanie and I both like cherry lollipops and Lucas and X‑Men and that we both want to be Rogue, even though she says we can’t really be Rogue. Maybe I’ll marry Anthony? Except he doesn’t talk to me. Melanie says her parents don’t talk either and that’s just being married. I don’t think I want that.

Dear Diary,
Dad says I don’t have to get married. Only if I want to. He said he and Mom used to talk every day. Then he got sad, but he said I can always ask about Mom. Sometimes it’s hard to miss someone you never met.

Dear Diary,
Kevin told me he likes me. He asked me to be his girlfriend. I asked what that means, and he said we’d eat lunch together and hold hands. I said I’d think about it. Melanie says say yes to make Anthony jealous, but Kevin is nice. I don’t want to be mean. Also, he ate a worm. Dad told me Mom’s favorite color was green, which is my favorite too, so I’m wearing my green shirt and pants tomorrow.

Dear Diary,
Kevin promised no more worms. So I said yes. We ate lunch together, but I missed Melanie. She ate with Tanya, which I didn’t like. Kevin gave me his chocolate cookie. After school, Anthony said bye to me. Maybe he is jealous. Everyone liked my green outfit. That made me happy.

Dear Diary,
On Sunday my frisbee landed in Dad’s boxes. They were full of Mom’s things. Pictures. Clothes. Scarves. It made Dad sad, but we put some things up. He told me he met Mom at Baskin‑Robbins. She always got mint chocolate chip. He let her buy him vanilla even though he doesn’t like ice cream because he liked her. She talked about unicorns in Ireland while his ice cream melted. He didn’t care. He liked watching her talk. There was a unicorn sweater in the box. Dad said I could have it. He said maybe we’ll go to Ireland for my 18th birthday.

Dear Diary,
I wore the unicorn sweater. Anthony liked it and asked me to be his girlfriend. I said no because I’m Kevin’s. But I don’t think I want to be Kevin’s either. I want to be Melanie’s. She smells nice and shares things. Melanie says we can’t be girlfriends, but we can be friends. So I’m no one’s girlfriend. Kevin was sad, then okay.

Dear Diary,
I dreamed Mom and I (and Lumpy) went to Ireland. A green unicorn flew us over a rainbow to Oz. Everything smelled like oranges. Dad says Mom had orange perfume. Today is Mom’s birthday. It’s mine too. We’re taking flowers to her grave and getting mint chocolate chip. I’m wearing the unicorn sweater again. It still smells a little like oranges.
